---
title: ""
pagetitle: "Table of Contents — Erika C. Freeman"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    page-layout: full
---

::: {.section-label}
Personal projects
:::
::: {.section-title}
table of contents
:::

A themed dinner series I run with Juli. Each edition takes one idea and teaches it across a multi-course, interactive meal: readings at the table, tasting experiments, and everyone cooking together. Vegan, alcohol-free paired. As the invitations put it, *the fruit is the pretext; you are the point.* **CITR·US** is the citrus strand.

::: {.log}

::: {.log-entry}
### CITR·US 1.0 — The Citrus Diaspora
[14 June 2026 · Zürich]{.log-meta}

Five courses following citrus along one branch of its six-thousand-year journey west, from Japan to Mexico. One human story, told in fruit.

- **Welcome · Japan** — yuzu highball
- **Vietnam** — gỏi bưởi, pomelo salad with herbs, peanuts, tamarind and lime
- **South India** — chitranna lemon rice, cucumber kachumber, coconut sambol
- **Morocco** — whole roast cauliflower with preserved lemon, harissa and tahini
- **Andalusia** — remojón orange salad, flatbread with bergamot syrup
- **Mexico** — vegan key lime pie with finger lime
:::

::: {.log-entry}
### CITR·US 2.0 — The Winter Sun
[Deep winter · for 11]{.log-meta}

Citrus as the fruit of the dark months: the one thing that ripens when the north needs colour, sharpness and vitamin C most. Five movements, served warm against the cold.

- **The cold-hardy one** — yuzu, and the winter-solstice bath
- **Blood and cold** — why winter nights turn the orange red (with a Moro-vs-navel tasting)
- **The Christmas orange** — when a single orange was treasure
- **The bitter preserve** — Seville, bergamot, and a midwinter marmalade flight
- **The sun returns** — vitamin C, scurvy, and the light coming back
:::

:::

::: {.personal-link}
[← Back to personal projects](personal.html)
:::
